nodes.name: Invoice Webhook
nodes.notes: Expects a multipart/form-data POST with the PDF under field name 'file'. Test with: curl -X POST -F file=@invoice.pdf http://localhost:5679/webhook/invoice-intake

nodes.name: Extract Invoice
nodes.notes: UNVERIFIED: this node's binary-file multipart configuration could not be tested against a live n8n instance. If it errors on import or execution, open it in the UI and manually confirm the Body Parameters section — the field name must be 'file' to match both the webhook's incoming binary property and the FastAPI service's expected parameter name.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { "status": "error", "stage": "extraction", "message": "Could not read the invoice — logged for review." } }}

nodes.name: Validate Invoice
nodes.notes: Passes the extraction result straight through as the request body — the validation service's ExtractedInvoice schema matches the extraction service's ExtractionResult schema field-for-field, so no reshaping needed.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { "status": "error", "stage": "validation", "message": "Extraction succeeded but validation failed — logged for review." } }}

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO invoices (source_filename, vendor_name, invoice_number, issue_date, total, outcome, flags, reasons, extracted_data)
VALUES (
  '{{ $json.source_filename.toString().replace(/'/g, "''") }}',
  {{ $json.vendor_name ? "'" + $json.vendor_name.toString().replace(/'/g, "''") + "'" : 'NULL' }},
  {{ $json.invoice_number ? "'" + $json.invoice_number.toString().replace(/'/g, "''") + "'" : 'NULL' }},
  {{ $json.issue_date ? "'" + $json.issue_date.toString().replace(/'/g, "''") + "'" : 'NULL' }},
  {{ $json.total !== null && $json.total !== undefined ? $json.total : 'NULL' }},
  '{{ $json.outcome }}',
  '{{ JSON.stringify($json.flags).replace(/'/g, "''") }}'::jsonb,
  '{{ JSON.stringify($json.reasons).replace(/'/g, "''") }}'::jsonb,
  '{{ JSON.stringify($json.extracted_data).replace(/'/g, "''") }}'::jsonb
)
RETURNING id;

nodes.name: Insert Invoice Record
nodes.notes: Manually quoted/escaped rather than using n8n's built-in Query Parameters field — that field has a known bug where any value containing a comma breaks positional parameter parsing, which we hit for real in Project 1.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { "status": "ok", "outcome": $('Merge Extraction + Validation').first().json.outcome, "flags": $('Merge Extraction + Validation').first().json.flags, "reasons": $('Merge Extraction + Validation').first().json.reasons } }}
